front.title: MARAM: Multi-layer Address Randomization and Authentication Mechanism

abstract.t: This document specifies MARAM (Multi-layer Address Randomization and Authentication Mechanism), an IPv6 extension that encrypts real endpoint addresses inside a Destination Option and uses ephemeral outer addresses with per-packet authentication. The key exchange is FYES (Fayazbakhsh, Yarmohammadi, Entezami, Shams), and a dedicated Vahed's Seal ensures protocol identification. The key derivation (FYES-KDF) uses domain separation strings for deriving independent keys.
